name: Chocolate Damnation
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Melt butter and semisweet chocolate in a saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat and stir in sugar, eggs, flour, baking powder, salt, and vanilla. Mix well.
3. Line a greased 15- x 10-inch jelly-roll pan with waxed paper. Pour the batter into the pan and spread evenly.
4. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched. Allow to cool on a rack.
5. Meanwhile, melt 12 oz semisweet chocolate and 1/4 cup coffee in a double boiler over hot water. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Beat egg yolks until pale yellow and stir in some of the melted chocolate mixture. Return the yolks to the chocolate and stir until smooth. Gradually add the coffee liqueur and mix well.
7. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add 3 tablespoons sugar and continue beating until stiff peaks form.
8. Whip the cream until stiff and fold into the cooled chocolate mixture. Gently fold in the beaten egg whites.
9. Invert the baked brownie shell onto a towel. Line the bottom and sides of a greased 9-inch square baking dish with the brownie shell, cutting strips for the sides and using the square for the bottom.
10. Pour the filling into the prepared cake-lined dish and cover with plastic wrap. Chill in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours or until firm.
11. To make the glaze, melt 4 oz semisweet chocolate and 3 tablespoons coffee in a double boiler over hot water.
12. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Invert the chilled dessert onto a serving platter and coat with the chocolate glaze. Allow the glaze to set before decorating with chocolate curls. Cut into thin slices to serve.
